sections_cited: Section 66 of the Sales Tax Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI CP D 4400 of 2021 Date Order with Signature of Judge(s) Hearing of case 1. For hearing of CMA No.18223/2021 2. For hearing of main case. 22-04-2025 Mr. Haider Waheed, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Irfan Mir Halepota, advocate for respondent Ms. Mahreen Ibrahim, Deputy Attorney General On the last date following order was passed:- Learned counsel for Respondent points out that the essential controversy is regarding sales tax refund. He refers to page 477, which is an order in respect of a refund application dated 05.04.2021, where a portion of claim was granted and a portion was disallowed. Learned counsel states that perusal of the order and the reference to the provision of law contained in the first paragraph demonstrates that this is an order under Section 66 of the Sales Tax Act. Learned counsel further states that the order is appealable per Section 45-B of Sales Tax Act. At this juncture, learned counsel for the petitioner seeks time to take instructions. To come up on 22.04.2025. Today learned counsel submits upon instructions that the petitioner will not press this petition and seek remedy before the forum of designated jurisdiction. He seeks that his appeal be adjudicated expeditiously and after providing an opportunity of hearing to the petitioner. Learned counsel for the respondent articulates no cavil to the aforesaid. In view hereof petition is dismissed as not pressed. Judge Judge Ayaz Gul
